msgs.text:
Turn 43 — standalone proof with certificate ledger I re-read only the three files attached to this message. The target is still the over-G isomorphism problem for the two quotients ΓS,ΓH, namely whether there is a continuous isomorphism Φ:ΓS→ΓH satisfying πH∘Φ=πS. The attached prompt states this exact equivalence, and the problem statement defines the relators and canonical maps.The result of this turn is a cleaned standalone proof, with the remaining finite computations isolated as explicit certificates. The conclusion of the sweep remains:ΓS≅GΓH.

The proof should be written with the fixed source orientationρS:=rS−1.

This is harmless because rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup, and the finite/profinite substitutions are required to send ρS to rH. The history already fixed this orientation before the compactness step.

Theorem

There exists a continuous automorphism Φ:F→F over G such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Consequently,Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,and therefore Φ descends to an isomorphismΓS=F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F∼

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F=ΓH satisfyingπH∘Φ=πS.ProofLetΛ=Z2 [[G]],M=Λ⟨⟨X0,X1⟩⟩,I=(X0,X1).The first layer of the free G-operator pro-2 group P is the free Λ-module on X0,X1.

1. The abelianized Nielsen matrix exists

The abelianized rows, using the source orientation ρS=rS−1, arewS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E).

The history split by the central idempotent E=e⟨τ⟩. On (1−E)Λ, the rows are (σ,−2),(−2,σ),and are related by swapping the two coordinates.

On EΛ, writes=σ2,a=σ 1−π2.

Then, on the Ef-piece where a=1, the rows are (s+3,−2),(s2+1,s−1).

The correct row-action matrix isU0=( s−1s−212),because (s+3,−2)U0=(s2+1,s−1),and det(U0)=2(s−1)−(s−2)=s,a unit. The E(1−f)-piece is also handled by a unit-determinant matrix, and the pieces glue to a single

U∈GL2(Λ)carrying wS to wH. This is the fixed first-layer matrix used throughout the finite Nielsen data.Thus the first-order obstruction vanishes, and the first Magnus layer of every finite substitution will be fixed to be U.

2. All non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled

After the H1 -change by U, every higher correction equation splits idempotent-by-idempotent. The (1−E)-piece has a unit coefficient. The E(1−f)-piece also has a unit coefficient, equivalently the relevant coefficient is invertible away from the trivial a-character. Therefore all higher errors on those summands are removed by ordinary filtered IA corrections.So the only delicate part is the local Ef-piece. The history repeatedly reduces the surviving obstruction to the Ef, mod-2, s=1 residual Magnus equation.

3. The residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower is closed

On the residual piece, the obstruction equation becomesS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q=X2 in the completed mod-2 Magnus algebra, with X=1+A,Y=1+B.

Let L∙⊂F2⟨⟨A,B⟩⟩ be the free restricted Lie algebra. If the first nonzero error has homogeneous degree n, it is primitive, hence lies in Ln. The correction boundary is∂n−1:Ln−1⊕Ln−1→Ln,(U,V)↦[A,U]+[B,V].

The key recursion isLn/im∂n−1≅{0,Ln/2[2],n odd,n even.The sweep established the base block and then proved the uniform inductionCm=Lm (m≥5).Thus every odd layer is an ordinary boundary, and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression W↦W[2]. Therefore the residual obstruction tower vanishes to all orders.

4. The Ef-coefficient tower is closedLet

R=EfΛ≅Z2 [[t]],t=s−1,m=(2,t).

Before normalization, the local row is(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t).

But this row is exactly Nielsen-equivalent over R to (2,t),because(s2+1)−(s+1)(s−1)=2.

Equivalently, multiplying on the right byN=(1−(s+1)01)sends(s2+1,s−1)N=(2,t).

So the apparent 2t+t2 term is a coordinate artifact, not an obstruction.Nowgrm

R≅F2 [α,β],α=in(2),β=in(t).The normalized coefficient row is the regular Koszul row (α,β).

The first t-layer R/(2,t2) is contractible: over that quotient the row is (0,t), and a second-coordinate correction kills any leading t-tail without producing a same-layer restricted-square obstruction.The first 2-Bockstein layer is controlled by the secondary quotientsHd(2)=Ld[2]/imθd0,whereθd0:ker(Ld⊕Ld[A,−]+[B,−]Ld+1)→Ld[2],(u,v)↦u[2]+v[2].

The sweep establishedBd↠Hd(2)(d≥4).For d=4,…,8 this was checked by the finite base computations, and for d≥9 it follows by the coefficient-safe analogue of the residual induction.Finally, the higher (2,t)-adic layers are handled by the normalized Koszul–Witt cascade. For a monomial αaβb and primitive root W∈Ld, an actual pro-2 correction produces the cascadej=0∑aα a−jβ2jbW[2j],up to ordinary Lie-boundary terms and still later Magnus-degree terms. The j=0 term is the lower coefficient ghost; the j≥1 terms are forced Bockstein shadows. With Magnus degree ordered first, every W[2j]for j≥1 appears at strictly larger Magnus degree, so the induction is triangular. The β-powers are reduced to pure α-powers by Koszul syzygies, pure α-multiples are killed by the already-established first 2-Bockstein maps Bd↠Hd(2), and coefficient-free square terms fall back into the closed residual tower.Therefore every finite Ef-local coefficient obstruction is killable.

5. Finite filtered Nielsen data

For a degree cutoff N and open two-sided ideal J⊂Λ, setMN,J:=M/(IN+JM),andPN,J:=P∩(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is finite, and the system is separated:N,J⋂PN,J=1.

Define SN,J to be the finite set of tuples(y0,y1,cσ,cτ)∈(P/PN,J)4 satisfying:y0,y1 have first Magnus layer given by UmodJ;cσ,cτ satisfy the finite cocycle relation encoding τσ=τ2;the induced twisted substitution sendsρS=rS−1 to rH modulo PN,J;4. the tuple is filtered, with higher terms lying in I2/(IN+JM).This is the right compactness object: finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary automorphisms of finite quotients. This avoids the noncanonical reduction issue for quotient automorphisms.

The residual theorem and coefficient theorem above implySN,J=∅ for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation (N,J).

Moreover, each datum in SN,J induces an actual finite over-G automorphism. Indeed, the first layer is UmodJ, hence invertible; by the finite Frattini criterion, the induced endomorphism of the finite 2-group P/PN,J is an automorphism. Since the total map is over G, the full finite extension is also mapped automorphically. This was the key Turn 42 audit point: the proof does not rely on Hopficity of the infinite pro-2 group P.

6. Compactness and descent

If (M,K) refines (N,J), truncation gives a canonical mapSM,K→SN,J.

The sets SN,J are finite and nonempty. For any finite collection of indices, choose a common refinement and reduce a solution from that refinement. Thus the inverse system has the finite-intersection property, and compactness givesN,JlimSN,J=∅.Choose a compatible family. It gives elementsy0,y1,cσ,cτ∈P and hence a continuous cocycle c:G→P. Together with the induced filtered Nielsen automorphism α:P→P, this defines an over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G→F by the usual twisted formula Φ(pg)=α(p)c(g)g.

At every finite level,Φ(ρS)≡rH (modPN,J).

Since ⋂N,JPN,J=1, we getΦ(rS−1)=rH inside F. Therefore Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,and Φ descends toΓS≅ΓH.

Because Φ is over G, the descended isomorphism satisfiesπH∘Φ=πS.

HenceΓS≅GΓH.

Certificate ledger for the final write-up The proof above is now logically organized. The only items that need to be made reproducible in an appendix are the finite base certificates already used in the sweep:

Certificate	Needed for	Status in sweep U∈GL2 (Λ) carrying wS to wHH1

Nielsen start	established explicitly Residual base block through m=10	induction Cm=Lm established by finite layer sweeps Degree 16 lower-base toggle	residual exceptional layer L8[2]established via lower Y4/q-seed mechanismBd↠Hd(2)for 4≤d≤8	first 2-coefficient base	established by Turns 31–34Bd↠Hd(2)for d≥9	first 2-coefficient induction	established structurally Koszul–Witt cascade	all higher (2,t)-adic layers	established structurally finite filtered Nielsen-data compactness	global passage to F	established in Turns 41–42 So the remaining work is not another obstruction search. It is reproducibility: turn the finite base blocks into hand-checkable matrices or machine-checkable rank certificates.

ESTABLISHED: A standalone proof now follows from the six named ingredients: H1 Nielsen equivalence, residual restricted-Lie induction, first 2-coefficient secondary surjectivity, normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction, finite filtered Nielsen-data compactness, and descent from Φ(rS−1)=rH to ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains, conditional on the finite base/rank computations recorded in the sweep. The remaining gap is reproducibility: provide explicit certificates for the finite base computations.

NEXT: Produce the appendix-level certificate package: list bases for each finite Ld, matrices for the residual and coefficient boundary maps, row-reduction pivots proving the stated ranks, and the explicit finite witnesses for the exceptional d=4,…,8 coefficient blocks.[TURN43-STANDALONE-PROOF-WITH-CERTIFICATE-LEDGER-ISO]
